title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers request smuggling, response splitting, parser differentials, and downgrades into a new multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Clte Tecl Defense
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 3142 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Clte Tecl Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reject transfer-encoding plus content-length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Clte Tecl Defense, keep Clte Tecl Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Downgrade Consistency Gate, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab protocol team handles case 3150 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Downgrade Consistency Gate, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A safe HTTP/2 front end guarantees the translated HTTP/1.1 backend request cannot be ambiguous.

Keep the plausible Downgrade Consistency Gate output and hide the violated multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Downgrade Consistency Gate.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
